working: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/19) is the standout: +66.5% session lift (baseline 17/day to 28.3/day) and it reaches the largest list at 9,731 sends.
Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3), the most recent send, posted +65.2% lift and a 46.25% open rate.
Open rates are uniformly strong: every send landed 42-56%, and the Constant Contact 100-campaign average open is 52.15%, far above the 25-35% nonprofit/health benchmark.
Referral grew vs last run (118 to 132 sessions, +11.9%) and Organic Social grew from 35 to 44 sessions (+25.7%), so the smaller channels are trending up.
Best click engagement came from Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2): 1.79% click rate and 3.77% click-to-open, the highest of the window.
not_working: UTM leakage is the dominant problem: all 8 sends show email_sessions_3d = 0 while each shows 21-53 direct_sessions_3d in the 3 days after send. Newsletter links carry no UTM tags, so GA4 mis-credits email clicks as Direct, which is inflating Direct to 51.3% of traffic.
Click rates are weak across the board: most sends sit at 0.4-1.2% click rate and ~1-2% click-to-open, below the CC 2.07% average and well under a healthy 10%+ CTOR.
Three sends had negative lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) at -50.2%, Staff-Client eNews - April (4/25) at -15.7%, and Alumni eNews - June (5/27) at -9.8%.
Organic Social is only 2.1% of sessions (44 of 2,059), below the 5% threshold worth counting as a channel.
No newsletter has gone out since 6/3 (three-week gap), and the 90-day window total slipped from 2,103 to 2,059 sessions (-2.1%) as the window rolled forward.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign per send) to every newsletter link. Until this is fixed Email reads as 0 attributed sessions despite 1,000-9,700 sends per blast, and Direct is overstated.

Resume the newsletter cadence: nothing has shipped since 6/3. The two highest-lift formats were the Staff-Client eNews (largest list, +66.5%) and Alumni reminder sends, so prioritize the next Staff-Client blast.
Repromote the themes that lifted sessions most: the Staff-Client eNews content and the Alumni 'Final Reminder' subject-line cadence, which together drove the +65-66% spikes.

On SEO, the GSC window shows 1,687 impressions but only 8 clicks (CTR ~0.5%) at an impression-weighted position of 18.48 (page 2). Push title/meta and on-page work on the near-page-1 queries to start converting impressions into clicks.
Mid-week sends performed best (Staff-Client 5/19 was a Tuesday); keep new sends to Tue-Wed mornings.
summary: All three sources (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC) are live. Direct (51.3%) and Organic Search (38.5%) carry traffic, but UTM-less newsletter links are mis-crediting email as Direct, and no send has gone out since 6/3 even though the best blasts lifted sessions 65-66%.
